steps.title: Find the core signal
steps.content: The key signal is that across all ten groups with different cultural backgrounds (urban dwellers, agricultural communities, hunter-gatherers), people rated odors identically—ethyl butyrate (peach) consistently more pleasant than mushroom alcohol (fungus).

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
If culture significantly influenced odor perception, different cultures would show different preferences.

But the team found identical preferences across all cultures, undermining this claim entirely.

The other choices address factors the study did not examine, describe what was demonstrated rather than undermined (C), or misstate the study's implications.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
